review:
I found out something was wrong when I tried to buy put options to protect my portfolio during a downturn. The order wouldn't go through. No warning. No notification. Nothing. I just couldn't trade.
Turns out Robinhood had quietly restricted my account because I travel internationally. No heads up. No email. No chance to prepare. They just silently pulled the rug while I was sitting on roughly $55K in positions.
So I'm watching the market fall and I can't do a single thing about it. I can't buy puts. I can't sell covered calls. I can't hedge. I can't even sell for tax loss harvesting. I had to ride the entire downturn completely naked with zero protection while Robinhood held my money hostage.
I contacted support. They told me to submit documentation proving my US residency. Fine. I sent them my lease agreement, bank statements, credit card statements. Everything they asked for.
Then they rejected it all. No explanation. They cited some regulation and essentially told me they don't have to justify their decision. So they asked me to jump through hoops, I jumped, and then they told me it doesn't matter.
I'm a US citizen. I have a US address. I gave them every document they requested. And they still won't let me manage my own money.
This isn't a brokerage. This is a trap. They'll take your deposits, they'll let you build a portfolio, and then the second their algorithm doesn't like something, they will cut you off from your own assets with no warning, no recourse, and no accountability. You will watch your money bleed while they ghost you.
If you have real money, get it out of Robinhood before they decide you don't deserve access to it anymore.

review:
I always begged for robinhood to be available since 2020 because the alternatives were just not as good and came with fees.

I used to use freetrade. 
To have an account worth £30,000 you would have to pay a fat FX fee on each trade Also price updating was a little slow and i always believed that I got a terrible entry even tho I bought exactly at a good price. Anyhow 

robinhood gives practically 0 fees. 0.03% to convert so for £30,000 it’s only £9. also i’m not aware too much about payment for order flow however the prices on market orders seems to be great?? They always give me the real true price of the stock. There’s a small stamp duty required not the government when you sell a stock but that seems to be around $0.30 for a $10,000 sell order so nothing to worry about. 

Since we’re in the uk. I had to consider the pattern day trader restriction. you cannot day trade or you’ll be restricted unless you have $25,000 or more in your portfolio.

All deposited money is kept in usd which is fine would be nice to let us by pounds with it especially sometimes i see the exchange rate looking favourable.

I’m regards to options trading im nearly sure it’s because uk laws. So fair enough. Would be good if you could prove you understand them/ prove you can handle the risks.

My realistic hope is you let uk users purchase uk stocks and ETF’s. We need $spy and $qqq badly. I really hope we can get Pershing square holding too.

Overall tho great app. Decent sign up bonus.
5% on usd is nice especially it being paid monthly.
After hours trading is just perfect seriously thank you guys.

review:
Be cautious about providing your NINO for verification. Under the updated MiFID II regulation, supplying a National Insurance number isn’t a mandatory requirement in the UK. Robinhood’s request for NINOs may reflect a cautious or standardised approach to KYC, likely influenced by US practices where SSN are commonly used for identification.

However, should a data breach occur, having provided such sensitive information could place you at greater risk. In today’s digital age, data breaches seem not a question of if but when. Despite my anticipation for Robinhood’s launch in the UK, I’ve decided against opening an account due to concerns over this requirement and the potential risk it entails.
